behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at the module handoff boundary (request builder -> API client payload). A unit test of a helper function can miss serialization/wiring mistakes; an in-process fake provider that captures outbound payload catches real failures. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only about payload contents, not real OS/network effects, not model output quality, and not multi-step agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API transport that records requests. Invoke classify with a taxonomy and assert the outbound payload contains every allowed label exactly as expected.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is response-handling across modules (API adapter output -> normalization/validation -> returned value). A stubbed API response is enough to trigger the real boundary failure. Rejected higher levels: behavior text focuses on invalid-label handling logic, not real network/OS behavior, not model-quality dependence, and no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical") and assert the public classify call returns "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure deterministic normalization logic on identical input; no module interaction or I/O is required to expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization directly with the same raw API payload multiple times and assert the exact same normalized label is produced each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live calls and consistency of a real AI provider's classification decisions. Stubbed responses would hide model-output variability and cannot validate this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls for the same document/taxonomy against the live provider, log raw provider outputs and final labels, and assert label stability when raw provider output is unchanged.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure is in wiring between config, budget enforcement, and dispatch gate. In-process test with a fake sender can verify that over-budget inputs are truncated/rejected before send. Rejected higher levels: behavior text says "before dispatch," so no real network/OS dependence; no dependence on AI output quality; no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small token budget, pass an over-budget document, and assert module applies configured policy (truncate or reject) and only dispatches compliant payloads to a fake transport.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This relies on real AI API behavior and provider-reported usage accounting. Mock usage values would trivially pass and miss real tokenizer/provider drift. Rejected Workflow: this is a single model call assertion, not a chained multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real provider calls for representative documents, read provider-reported token usage from responses, and assert usage does not exceed configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is retry orchestration across rate-limit error handling, wait policy, and re-dispatch. A fake API that first returns rate-limit then success catches this without real network. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not require real OS/network primitives, does not depend on model output quality, and is not a multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client scripted to return rate-limit on first call and success on second; assert classifier waits per policy, retries, and returns successful classification instead of surfacing an error.
